subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand how humans adapt to different environments
Learn how environment influences settlements, agriculture, and trade
Explore examples of adaptation in mountains, plains, deserts, and coasts
Recognize the impact of natural disasters on communities
Develop observation and problem-solving skills related to environment

lesson_plan.title: Humans and Their Environment
topics_covered: How humans live in different regions
student_activities: Look at pictures of different environments
Discuss how people live in mountains, deserts, and coasts
Share one example from their local area

lesson_plan.title: Adaptation and Settlement
topics_covered: How people adapt homes and clothing to climate
Settlements in hills, plains, and coasts
teaching_strategy: Show images of houses in mountains, deserts, and coastal areas
Ask students how people survive in these conditions
student_activities: Match environment with type of house

lesson_plan.title: Agriculture in Different Regions
topics_covered: Farming in plains, hills, and deserts
teaching_strategy: Show pictures of farms and terraces
Discuss crops grown in different regions
student_activities: Draw a small farm suited to a chosen region

lesson_plan.title: Trade and Transportation
topics_covered: How rivers, coasts, and passes help trade
Goods commonly traded in different regions
teaching_strategy: Use simple props to demonstrate trading goods
student_activities: Identify why trade routes follow rivers or coasts

lesson_plan.title: Natural Disasters and Human Life
topics_covered: How people adapt and prepare for disasters
teaching_strategy: Show images and short videos of disasters
student_activities: Identify disasters common in their region
Discuss what people can do to stay safe

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Quiz
topics_covered: Relate examples to students’ own lives
teaching_strategy: Fun quiz with pictures and multiple choice
student_activities: Draw or share one thing they learned about human adaptation

teaching_aids: Pictures of different environments and settlements
Maps and charts for visual learning
Props for trade and farming activities
teacher_tip: Keep lessons visual and interactive. Use examples from local environments. Encourage students to think how humans adapt and modify their surroundings.
